why: Cargo packages follow Kernel, platform and API ownership.
Several Kernel and development runtime root directories still encode responsibility in file prefixes.
Most high-churn areas are already split by responsibility.
Development runtime and three Kernel namespaces are still flat enough to slow navigation.
Public crate roots are explicit and platform protocol is separate from Kernel implementation.
The approved moves are private-module moves with no new dependency edges.
Control Store and Vault use named responsibility directories.
Identity, module control and runtime lifecycle files require prefixes instead of namespaces.
Kernel, API and platform packages are separate Cargo owners.
This pass changes only filesystem module grouping.
All proposed Kernel and development moves are private.
Events retains its public crate-root export explicitly.
Existing folders use meaningful ownership names.
Flat names such as registration_ipc and child_supervisor indicate missing navigation namespaces.
No new crates or dependencies are proposed.
Module import rewrites are declared explicitly in the plan.
Vault and Control Store are already decomposed.
Kernel source still needs three local namespace cuts and the development operator needs four.
